name: Mark's Famous Po-Boy
recipeInstructions:
1. For best results, use slightly frozen roast beef to achieve thin slices.
2. Place the sliced roast in a bowl with worcestershire sauce and marinate for 15 to 20 minutes.
3. In a skillet, sauté onions, bell peppers, and half a teaspoon of seasoning salt in peanut oil until tender.
4. Remove the vegetables from the skillet and add the meat back in. Cook until just browned, then add the remaining seasoning salt.
5. Add the cooked vegetables to the meat and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
6. Place French bread open-faced under a broiler until crispy.
7. Arrange the meat mixture on top of the bread, cover with Swiss cheese slices, and broil until the cheese melts.
8. Spread mayonnaise over the sandwich and serve with any remaining juices.
